up.method: measured biomass-specific consumption rate, exponential phase (+/-0.70)

sec.method: measured biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase (+/-0.84)

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: the cells displayed exponential growth with a growth rate of 1.56 (+/- 0.10) h-1 ... Wolfe's medium (16.1 +/- 0.70 mmol gDW-1 h-1) ... acetate production rates ... 7.11 (+/- 0.84) mmol gDW-1 h-1 in Wolfe's medium
growth_records.notes: Fig.1 batch bioreactor, original Wolfe's medium at 68 C, pH 7, 10 g/L glucose. mu=1.56+/-0.10 h-1 -> td=ln2/1.56=0.44 h. Acetate sole byproduct in aerobic exponential phase. After N-depletion at 1 g DW/L additional byproducts (pyruvate, trace lactate) appear.

growth_records.medium: Defined medium supplemented with full Wolfe's vitamins (Fig.1 defined medium)

up.method: measured biomass-specific consumption rate, exponential phase (+/-1.26)

sec.method: measured biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase (+/-0.97)

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: the biomass specific glucose consumption rate in the exponential phase was higher in the defined medium (25.5 +/- 1.26 mmol gDW-1 h-1) ... acetate production rates of 9.94 (+/- 0.97) mmol gDW-1 h-1 in the defined medium
growth_records.notes: Fig.1 batch bioreactor, intermediate defined medium (all Wolfe's vitamins) vs Wolfe's, 68 C pH 7, 10 g/L glucose. qGlc=25.5+/-1.26, qAcetate=9.94+/-0.97 (exponential phase). Growth rate for this run stated only qualitatively as higher than Wolfe's 1.56; numeric mu not given for Fig.1 defined medium (the 2.20 h-1 headline value belongs to final MLGM run, separate record). Intermediate medium not tabulated -> composition stated false.

growth_records.medium: Defined medium supplemented with biotin and vitamin B12 only (Fig.3)

up.method: measured biomass-specific consumption rate, exponential phase (+/-1.1)

sec.method: measured biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase (+/-0.2)

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: supplementation with only biotin and vitamin B12 resulted in a maximum biomass specific growth rate of 1.76 (+/- 0.05) h-1 ... acetate production rate of 8.9 (+/- 0.2) mmol gDW-1 h-1, and glucose was consumed with a specific rate of 25.8 (+
growth_records.notes: Fig.3 batch bioreactor, defined medium with only biotin+B12, 68 C pH 7, glucose. mu=1.76+/-0.05 -> td=ln2/1.76=0.39 h. qGlc=25.8+/-1.1, qAcetate=8.9+/-0.2 (acetate sole byproduct). Predates MOPS-buffer switch that later raised mu ~15% toward final MLGM value of 2.20 h-1.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM), final optimized defined medium

sec.method: measured maximum biomass-specific production rate, sole byproduct before O2 limitation (+/-0.56)

yields.Yxs: 0.35 g DW / g glucose (fully aerobic phase); 0.30 g DW / g glucose (overall, at carbon depletion)

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: the maximum biomass specific growth rate was 2.20 (+/- 0.04) h-1 ... biomass yield during this fully aerobic phase was 0.35 g DW g glc-1 ... overall biomass yield on glucose of 0.30 g DW g glc-1. The glucose was consumed with a maximum biom
growth_records.notes: Final MLGM (Table 2) batch bioreactor, 68 C, pH 7, 10 g/L glucose, aerobic. mu=2.20+/-0.04 h-1 (authors: fastest ever reported in a defined medium) -> td=ln2/2.20=0.32 h. qGlc(max)=35.7+/-1.1, qAcetate(max)=10.1+/-0.56 (acetate sole byproduct before O2 limitation). Yxs=0.35 g DW/g glc (aerobic phase), 0.30 g DW/g glc overall. Distinct from Fig.1 defined-medium run (qGlc 25.5) and Fig.3 biotin+B12 run (mu 1.76) due to MOPS buffer + iron/trace optimization.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with xylose
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Achieved growth rates and yields on different carbon sources ... Xylose 1.25 (+/- 0.01)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C on MLGM. mu(xylose)=1.25+/-0.01 -> td=ln2/1.25=0.55 h. Carbon-source concentration not specified per source (glucose reference 10 g/L). No uptake/secretion rates in this screen. pH not stated for shake-flask screen (MLGM uses 50 mM MOPS, initial pH ~6.9-7).

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with galactose
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(galactose)=0.72+/-0.03 -> td=ln2/0.72=0.96 h. Composition stated false to avoid implying galactose concentration; base = MLGM Table 2. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with maltose
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(maltose)=0.76+/-0.01 -> td=ln2/0.76=0.91 h. Authors attribute maltose uptake to promiscuous transporter activity. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with sucrose
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(sucrose)=1.91+/-0.01 -> td=ln2/1.91=0.36 h. Both glucose and fructose moieties dissimilated. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with cellobiose
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(cellobiose)=1.89+/-0.05 -> td=ln2/1.89=0.37 h. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with soluble starch
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(starch)=1.42+/-0.06 -> td=ln2/1.42=0.49 h. No annotated alpha-amylase in genome despite growth. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with mannitol
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C. mu(mannitol)=1.56+/-0.04 -> td=ln2/1.56=0.44 h. No rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with glycerol
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Glycerol 1.95 (+/- 0.002) ... Glycerol data were collected from duplicate bioreactor cultivations ... the highest ever reported growth rate in defined medium on glucose (2.20 h-1) or glycerol (1.95 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1, glycerol from duplicate BATCH BIOREACTOR cultivations (not shake flask) on defined MLGM, 68 C, pH 7. mu(glycerol)=1.95+/-0.002 -> td=ln2/1.95=0.36 h; authors state highest ever reported on glycerol. No uptake/secretion rates given for glycerol.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM) with acetate
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist G et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 carbon-source screen, triplicate shake flasks 68 C, acetate sole carbon source. mu(acetate)=0.69+/-0.02 -> td=ln2/0.69=1.00 h. No growth on rhamnose, arabinose, fructose, lactose or citrate. No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Swarup et al. 2014 defined minimal medium (glucose)

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H) substrate depletion during logarithmic phase, biomass by CDW

sec.method: HPLC (diode array 210 nm); sole by-product during log phase on glucose

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist E et al. Genome-scale reconstruction and metabolic modelling of the fast-growing thermophile Geobacillus sp. LC300. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2022.e00212. [PMC9678985]
growth_records.snippet: Glucose | mu 1.56 (+-0.10) | Substrate 16.05 (+-0.70) | Acetate 7.11 (+-0.84) | Oxygen 34.41 (+-3.85) | Carbon dioxide 24.33 (+-0.33). Rates are maximum biomass-specific consumption/production rates (mmol gDW-1 h-1) determined during logari
growth_records.notes: Table 2, experimental quadruplicate bioreactor batch at 68C. mu and substrate/acetate = mean of 4 cultures; O2/CO2 = mean of duplicate off-gas measurements. Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.56=0.444 h (26.7 min). Yxs=mu/qS=1.56/16.05=0.0972 gDW/mmol; per g: 0.0972/0.18016 g/mmol=0.540 gDW/g glucose. Acetate yield=7.11/16.05=0.443 mmol/mmol. RQ=qCO2/qO2=24.33/34.41=0.707. Model iGEL604 pFBA predicted mu 1.51 vs experimental 1.56 (predicted values not curated as experimental). A 13C-MFA intracellular flux comparison (Fig.3) uses fluxes CITED from Cordova & Antoniewicz 2016 and Cordova et al. 2017 (LC300 at 72C), not measured in this paper; therefore mfa_fluxes left empty here.

growth_records.medium: Swarup et al. 2014 defined minimal medium (xylose)

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H) substrate depletion during logarithmic phase, biomass by CDW

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist E et al. Genome-scale reconstruction and metabolic modelling of the fast-growing thermophile Geobacillus sp. LC300. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2022.e00212. [PMC9678985]
growth_records.snippet: Xylose | mu 1.07 (+-0.06) | Substrate 13.61 (+-0.60) | Acetate - | Oxygen 32.74 (+-1.21) | Carbon dioxide 31.81 (+-2.82). During the logarithmic growth phase on xylose no by-products were found, although acetate was produced in the early st
growth_records.notes: Table 2, experimental quadruplicate bioreactor batch at 68C on xylose. No acetate secretion during log phase (acetate only in early stationary phase), so no secretion rate reported for acetate. td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.07=0.648 h (38.9 min). Yxs=mu/qS=1.07/13.61=0.0786 gDW/mmol; per g: 0.0786/0.15013 g/mmol=0.524 gDW/g xylose. RQ=qCO2/qO2=31.81/32.74=0.972. Model pFBA predicted mu 1.00 vs experimental 1.07 (prediction not curated). 13C-MFA comparison fluxes are cited from Cordova et al., not measured here.

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
